title: Remove Loop from Linked List
hints: Use Floyd's cycle detection algorithm (slow and fast pointers) to detect if there's a cycle.
If a cycle is detected, find the start of the cycle by moving one pointer from head and one from meeting point.
Once you find the start of the cycle, traverse to find the node just before the start of the cycle.
Set the next pointer of the node before the cycle start to null to break the loop.
If no cycle is detected, return the list as is.
